title: buddleja loniceroides
intro: Buddleja loniceroides, also known as the butterfly bush, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern region of North America, characterized by its fragrant, white flowers and gray-green leaves. Traditionally, Buddleja loniceroides has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing sore throats, and promoting digestion. Buddleja loniceroides can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and asthma.
